content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
March 4, 1982 
Ms. Pam Swift, City Clerk 
City of Pasadena 
Room 218 
100 North Garfield Avenue 
Pasadena, C~ 91109 
Dear Pam: 
Enforcement 
322-64.41 
Statements of Economic I nteres 
322-6444 
This letter is to confirm my advice to you during our telephone 
conversation of January 26, 1982. 
Please accept my apologies for the delay in providing you with 
this letter. 
You asked whether donations solicited by a councilmember specifically 
to be used for a newsletter for his or her constituency are report-
able under the Political Reform Act. 
At this time the councilmember 
plans to issue the newsletter on a regular basis which will be deter-
mined by the amount of donations received. 
7he newsletter will con-
tain articles on issues concerning the city and will not carry any 
"political purposes" articles on any other officeholders, ballot 
measures, candidates, referendums or initiatives. 
Monies received 
will be maintained in a separate account and will not be commingled 
with the campaign funds of the officeholder. 
I advised you that since the donations solicited for the newsletter 
will not be received for "political purposes," that is, to influence 
the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election 
of any officeholder, candidate, measure, or the qualification of 
any measure (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225) I 
these dona-
tions are not required to be reported on any campaign statement. 
I hope this information has been helpful to you. 
Please contact me 
if I can be of further assistance. 
/"] j. 
/h.<:~~~<: /c:( 
Helen P. A.rriola 
Political Reform Consultant 
HPA:cm

sections.question: You asked whether donations solicited by a councilmember specifically to be used for a newsletter for his or her constituency are reportable under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: I advised you that since the donations solicited for the newsletter will not be received for "political purposes," that is, to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of any officeholder, candidate, measure, or the qualification of any measure (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225), these donations are not required to be reported on any campaign statement.
sections.question_synthetic: Are donations solicited by a councilmember specifically for a constituency newsletter reportable under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Donations for the newsletter are not reportable under the Political Reform Act because they are not received for "political purposes" as defined by law. Provided the newsletter contains only city issue articles and not articles about other officeholders, ballot measures, candidates, or initiatives, and funds are kept separate from campaign funds, reporting is not required.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter confirming prior telephone consultation. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Document references specific California Administrative Code sections (18215 and 18225). Minor formatting irregularities in original document do not affect extraction quality.

QUESTION: You asked whether donations solicited by a councilmember specifically to be used for a newsletter for his or her constituency are reportable under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: I advised you that since the donations solicited for the newsletter will not be received for "political purposes," that is, to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of any officeholder, candidate, measure, or the qualification of any measure (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225), these donations are not required to be reported on any campaign statement.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms that donations solicited by a councilmember for a constituency newsletter are not reportable under the Political Reform Act, as they are not received for political purposes as defined in the statute.
